C[Garp: What you first need to master are Joseki.
I would first suggest you to get Kogos dictionary:
At first, just follow them when you play online. Try to memorise the simpliest one (know at least one answer to any classical approach).
Then you'll learn which one to use depending on conditions (have some stone you can use as a follow-up, etc.).
After that, you'll study them: Joseki are the *best* possible move according to 9dan pro: trying to understand *why* will definitely improve your level.
Here your answer is not a classical Joseki answer: I'm not a 9dan, and I cant tell you why, but I'm sure you lose some points on that move.
C[Garp: This move allow a ladder that will be developed in the few next moves.
A ladder is fatal if it ends on a wall or on an opponent stone.
C[Garp: Standard ladder move would have been W H19.
C[Garp: There (or around) you should have moved first. Black has a strong wall o the top allowing a powerful deployment on the left. W C12 partially block this opportunity.

C[Garp: This move doesnâ€™t gives you much. Each move should be chosen as maximizing the territories you get by it. At the beginning of the game, you can get 10-20 stones by move. That one is far from being that efficient.
C[Garp: You now have a powerful influence on the bottom. I think this is winning moves for you.
C[Garp: This move doesnt help much.
C[Garp: The goal is to make territories, not walls. That stone is again useless for that.
C[Garp: A lot of stones for a quite small result: again, every move has to maximize the territory you'll get.
C[Garp: O6 would have been better.
C[Garp: Too late and a bit weak. O11 would have secure some area.
C[Garp: N9 L11 is what is called an ""elephant step"": the middle point m10 is very weak and a point of attack. Try to avoid that shape.
C[Garp: All  those moves didnt do much. would have been better to extend  and build your tow  groups at the bottom.
C[Garp: The work wasnt finished B P4 was urgent.
C[N3rdism: That would probably be best, thanks
